creator: thebeautydoctrine
description: Luxury skincare prices do not always reflect better formulations.  Skinceuticals C E Ferulic is one of the most famous vitamin C serums on the market, but it costs about $185 and relies on pure ascorbic acid at a very low pH. This type of vitamin C can be effective but is also known for causing irritation, dryness, and burning in many users, which is reflected in a surprising number of negative reviews. When you compare the ingredient structure to more affordable formulas, the difference becomes interesting.  Seoul Ceuticals uses sodium ascorbyl phosphate, a more stable and gentler vitamin C derivative, combined with MSM, centella, aloe, and ferulic acid to support skin repair and antioxidant protection.  At under $20, it raises a fair question about whether luxury pricing in skincare always reflects superior formulation.  #tiktokshopcreatorpicks #greenish #skincarecomparison #vitamincserum #koreanskincare

description: Replying to @mikaylee-tiktok shop affiliate  Why we made TWO different kids’ toothpastes 🦷 A lot of parents ask why our red cap and yellow cap kids toothpastes are different. It’s actually intentional — because a toddler’s mouth and a 6-year-old’s mouth are not the same. Red Cap (Ages 1+) – Toddler Formula • Made with 10% xylitol to help reduce cavity-causing bacteria • Very simple glycerin-based formula • Designed to be gentle and safe if swallowed • Ideal for toddlers who are just learning to brush and don’t spit well yet Yellow Cap (Ages 6+) – Advanced Kids Formula • Hydroxyapatite to help support enamel remineralization • 25% xylitol for stronger cavity protection • Still fluoride-free and free of SLS, parabens, dyes, and artificial sweeteners • Designed for kids who are starting to get permanent teeth Around age 6, children begin developing their adult teeth, so oral care needs change. That’s why we upgraded the formula — not just the flavor. Red cap = gentle starter toothpaste Yellow cap = stronger protection for growing smiles Same mission at GuruNanda: Clean, thoughtful formulas for every stage of life. #GuruNanda #KidsToothpaste #Xylitol #Hydroxyapatite #oralcareforkids

creator: jaxster.com
description: #stitch with @smilinggg:) These Medicube Red Succinic acid pads have helped many with their inflamed acne and cystic acne. 🧪 1. Gentle chemical exfoliation unclogs pores 	•	The pads contain salicylic acid (BHA), a gold-standard acne-fighting ingredient. It penetrates into pores, dissolves dead skin and sebum, and helps prevent clogged pores that lead to breakouts. BHA also has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm angry, puffy blemishes.   ⚖️ 2. Succinic acid helps with acne-related inflammation 	•	Succinic acid is included because it’s shown to help control acne-related bacteria and reduce localized inflammation. It doesn’t exfoliate as strongly as BHAs/AHAs but supports the acne-targeting effect and may help reduce the size/redness of cyst-like spots.   🔥 3. Niacinamide reduces redness and strengthens skin barrier 	•	Niacinamide (Vitamin B3) supports the skin’s barrier function and may reduce redness and irritation that often accompanies inflamed acne and sensitive skin. A stronger barrier can help skin handle irritation better, including mild rosacea flare-ups.   💧 4. Panthenol & soothing ingredients calm irritation 	•	Panthenol (pro-vitamin B5) and other soothing extracts in the formula help replenish moisture and calm irritation after exfoliation. This is important because exfoliating acids can be irritating if left unsupported by calming and hydrating components.   🔄 5. Improving cell turnover helps with texture and scarring 	•	Regular, gentle exfoliation promotes faster skin turnover, which can fade post-acne marks and texture irregularities over time. This doesn’t directly treat rosacea itself but can help visibly refine skin that’s been inflamed.  #acne#acnet#cysticacnea#rosacean#skincare

creator: jaxster.com
description: Advanced Level Chemical Exfoliant. 30% AHA 2% BHA. The Ordinary AHA 30% + BHA 2% Peeling Solution is an advanced-level chemical exfoliant that works by dissolving the "glue" that holds dead skin cells together on the surface and inside the pores. It is highly effective for improving skin radiance, refining texture, and clearing pore congestion, but it is formulated only for experienced users of acid exfoliation due to its high acid concentration.  The Ordinary The Ordinary  +2 How the Peeling Solution Works The formula combines two main types of hydroxy acids that target different layers of the skin: Alpha Hydroxy Acids (AHAs) - 30%: A blend of glycolic, lactic, tartaric, and citric acids that exfoliate the skin's topmost surface. By removing these superficial layers, it helps fade sun damage, smooth uneven texture, and reduce the appearance of fine lines. Beta Hydroxy Acid (BHA) - 2%: Specifically salicylic acid, which is oil-soluble. Unlike AHAs, BHAs can penetrate into the pores to dissolve sebum and debris, effectively reducing congestion that leads to blemishes, blackheads, and whiteheads.  Key Supporting Ingredients To balance the high acidity (pH ~3.6), the formula includes soothing and hydrating agents: Tasmanian Pepperberry: A plant-derived extract that helps reduce the irritation and redness often associated with strong acid use. Hyaluronic Acid Crosspolymer: Provides a comfortable skin feel and post-exfoliation hydration. Vitamin B5: Supports skin healing and enhances surface hydration. Black Carrot: Acts as an antioxidant for additional skin protection.  The Ordinary The Ordinary  +2 Effectiveness and Results User reviews and editorial tests consistently rate the product highly, often averaging 4.7 out of 5 stars.  Immediate Impact: Users frequently report "baby butt smooth" skin and a notable increase in radiance immediately after the first 10-minute treatment. Texture and Tone: With continued use (ideally once a week), it effectively "blitzes" small spots, flattens whiteheads, and refines the appearance of pores. Professional-Grade at Home: It is considered one of the closest at-home alternatives to a professional dermatologist's peel due to its 32% total acid concentration.  Reddit Reddit  +3 Essential Safety Guidelines Because of its potency, this product must be used with extreme caution: Usage Limits: Use only in the evening, no more than twice a week. Time Limit: Never leave it on for longer than 10 minutes. Application: Apply only to thoroughly dry, clean skin. Do not use on broken, sensitive, or peeling skin. Sun Sensitivity: AHAs significantly increase sun sensitivity; daily sunscreen use is mandatory during use and for one week after stopping. Incompatibilities: Do not use in the same routine as other direct acids, retinoids (Vitamin A), or Vitamin C #beauty #skincare #koreanskincare #face #hyperpigmentation
